channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Untold casualties and humanitarian needs: What to know a week from Venezuela's quakes
item.description: Here's a look at some of the major developments since major back-to-back earthquakes rocked Venezuela on June 24, devastating parts of a country already reeling from crisis after crisis.
item.content:encoded:
Here's a look at some of the major developments since major back-to-back earthquakes rocked Venezuela on June 24, devastating parts of a country already reeling from crisis after crisis.

(Image credit: Ariana Cubillos)
item.dc:creator: Fatima Al-Kassab

item.title: Lone star ticks are covering much of the U.S. Here's what you need to know
item.description: It's a tick that hunts you down and transmits a potentially dangerous allergy to red meat. The New Yorker writer Burkhard Bilger discusses the lone star tick and the risks of alpha-gal syndrome.
item.content:encoded: It's a tick that hunts you down and transmits a potentially dangerous allergy to red meat. The New Yorker writer Burkhard Bilger discusses the lone star tick and the risks of alpha-gal syndrome.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Americans are showing up for the World Cup in record-breaking numbers
item.description: While the U.S. isn't a bona fide soccer nation yet, the past three weeks have perhaps shown what it would feel like if it were.
item.content:encoded:
While the U.S. isn't a bona fide soccer nation yet, the past three weeks have perhaps shown what it would feel like if it were.

(Image credit: Jacob Lujan)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Funerals held for 14 Pakistani children killed in tutoring center collapse
item.description: Police are investigating whether negligence during construction work caused the collapse in the eastern city of Lahore on Tuesday.
item.content:encoded:
Police are investigating whether negligence during construction work caused the collapse in the eastern city of Lahore on Tuesday.

(Image credit: K.M. Chaudary)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: SCOTUS upholds birthright citizenship. And, key results from Colorado's primaries
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld birthright citizenship on constitutional grounds on the last day of its term. And, a Democratic socialist has won the Democratic primary for a U.S. House seat in Denver.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court upheld birthright citizenship on constitutional grounds on the last day of its term. And, a Democratic socialist has won the Democratic primary for a U.S. House seat in Denver.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Greetings from London, where Banksy's flag man is a warning cry
item.description: As he marches on, his flag blows back into his face, leaving him unaware he is only a step away from a perilous fall. As usual with Banksy's art, the statue presents a sharp critique of society.
item.content:encoded: As he marches on, his flag blows back into his face, leaving him unaware he is only a step away from a perilous fall. As usual with Banksy's art, the statue presents a sharp critique of society.
item.dc:creator: Vincent Ni

item.title: A federal law bans late voter roll purges. Republicans are pushing to reinterpret it
item.description: There's a ban on most states systematically purging voter rolls within 90 days before Election Day. Republicans are pushing courts to reinterpret that longstanding protection for eligible voters.
item.content:encoded:
There's a ban on most states systematically purging voter rolls within 90 days before Election Day. Republicans are pushing courts to reinterpret that longstanding protection for eligible voters.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: How America has celebrated milestone birthdays, from world fairs to the World Cup
item.description: Two founding fathers died on America's 50th birthday, which was marked with toasts and parades. Over time, anniversary celebrations became more extravagant — and more controversial.
item.content:encoded:
Two founding fathers died on America's 50th birthday, which was marked with toasts and parades. Over time, anniversary celebrations became more extravagant — and more controversial.

(Image credit: Hulton Archive)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: 5 safety tips to keep you out of the emergency room this summer
item.description: Experts share guidance on how to prevent common summertime health risks, such as drowning, fire accidents and heat exhaustion.
item.content:encoded: Experts share guidance on how to prevent common summertime health risks, such as drowning, fire accidents and heat exhaustion.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Supreme Court deals some blows to Trump's agenda but leaves him with more expansive powers
item.description: President Trump has trumpeted his victories and sought workarounds for his losses.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has trumpeted his victories and sought workarounds for his losses.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith
